selected_items.title: Protein ceiling myth: deuterium tax, metabolic water collapse, and singlet oxygen trap in high-protein diets
selected_items.why_interesting: Long numbered thread connecting a 2026 protein study to deuterium isotope effects, metabolic water production, dielectric collapse, kinetic isotope stall, and singlet oxygen damage. Introduces Blau Lab (2026) mechanistic link between microgravity muscle wasting and GLP-1/Ozempic lattice lock via 15-PGDH gerozyme and PGE2 repair signal.
selected_items.source_claim: A 2026 study showing 100g protein sustains muscle protein synthesis for 12 hours is misinterpreted by centralized biochemistry. The real mechanism: high protein intake cuts metabolic water production in half (vs. fat-derived CCO water), collapses the Exclusion Zone dielectric constant from ~160 to ~78, traps deuterium in Inner Mitochondrial Junctions, stalls Complex I via kinetic isotope effect, loses CISS (Chiral Induced Spin Selectivity), converts triplet oxygen to destructive singlet oxygen (1O2), and causes hair loss, neural damage, and entropy-driven tissue incineration. The Blau Lab's discovery of 15-PGDH as a gerozyme destroying PGE2 repair signal is the missing mechanistic bridge.
selected_items.mechanism: High protein intake (100g) reduces metabolic water production by ~50% compared to fat oxidation. CCO normally manufactures 110g deuterium-depleted water per 100g fat substrate (dielectric constant ε≈160). Protein metabolism demands water for urea cycle ammonia clearance, creating intracellular desiccation. K+-stabilized Exclusion Zone collapses; dielectric constant sags to bulk water (~78). Deuterium accumulates in IMJs, invading ATPase nano-motors. Kinetic isotope effect stalls at Complex I, destroying NAD+ turnover (NAD+ → NADD+). Loss of CISS effect allows triplet oxygen to become singlet oxygen (1O2), a thermal 'hot potato' that denatures proteins, fries hair follicles, yellows teeth, and damages neurons via entropy heat loss from neuroectoderm.
selected_items.reader_change: Recognize that high protein intake (especially 100g+ per meal) may cause hidden desiccation, deuterium accumulation, and singlet oxygen damage despite apparent muscle synthesis gains. Watch for hair loss on top of head, cognitive decline, and yellowed teeth as early warning signs. Consider protein ceiling limits and deuterium-depleted water (DDW) or cold thermogenesis (CT) to expand EZ water layers and restore dielectric coherence.
support_quotes: When the body combusts fat via beta-oxidation, Cytochrome c Oxidase (CCO) manufactures 110 grams of perfectly structured, deuterium-depleted water per 100g of substrate. This high-dielectric fluid (𝜖 ≈160) acts as the frictionless lubricant for the Brachistochrone curve of the cristae. By contrast, ingesting 100g of protein cuts metabolic water production exactly in half.

The accumulating deuterium creates a massive KIE stall at Complex I. The system is forced into a state of pseudohypoxia and this destroys NAD+ turining it into NADD+. Because the water lattice is deuterated, the cell cannot maintain the CISS effect (Chiral Induced Spin Selectivity). When CISS is lost triplet oxygen becomes singlet and singlet oxygen is what causes hair loss, weaker collagen, and neural loss.
Singlet state destroys tissue coherence and fuels entropy in heat loss that fries hair follicles and brain cells. It will also yellow your teeth.

source_citations.claim: Establishes mathematical hierarchy of metabolic water production during complete combustion; subatomic proof that 100g protein ceiling study ignores deuterium tax and water table collapse

selected_items.scientific_translation_plan: Explain CCO as the mitochondrial enzyme that burns fat and produces water; deuterium-depleted water as structured, coherent water vs. bulk water. Explain dielectric constant as a measure of how well water holds electrical charge; EZ as Gerald Pollack's exclusion zone of highly ordered water. Explain kinetic isotope effect as the slowing of chemical reactions when heavy hydrogen (deuterium) replaces normal hydrogen. Explain CISS as the quantum property that allows electrons to 'choose' spin states based on molecular chirality. Explain singlet vs. triplet oxygen as two quantum spin states of oxygen; singlet is highly reactive and damaging. Explain gerozyme as an enzyme that promotes aging. Explain the mechanism: high protein intake → reduced metabolic water → collapsed EZ → deuterium trapping → KIE stall → loss of spin selectivity → singlet oxygen damage → tissue and neural harm.
selected_items.risk_notes: This is a long, mechanistically dense thread with novel claims about protein metabolism, deuterium, and quantum biology. The Blau Lab 2026 citation is recent and should be verified. The Mellanby 1942 paper is historical and should be checked. The mechanism linking protein intake to singlet oxygen via deuterium and CISS is Kruse's original synthesis and is not mainstream biochemistry; the writer must present it as Kruse's framework, not as established science. The claim that hair loss and cognitive decline are early signs of singlet oxygen damage is speculative and should be framed as Kruse's hypothesis.

selected_items.title: RETA (retatrutide) complications: mechanoporation, TBI-like shock waves, CPM mimicry, and water table biophysics
selected_items.why_interesting: Jack predicts that RETA (retatrutide, a GLP-1/GIP/glucagon triple agonist not yet FDA-approved) will cause mechanoporation-induced TBI-like damage and central pontine myelinolysis (CPM) mimicry in users with deuterated water tables. Claims decentralized neurosurgery can treat complications by reestablishing water table biophysics.
selected_items.source_claim: RETA will cause mechanical shock wave damage via mechanoporation that mimics traumatic brain injury (TBI) impacts. Users with deuterated water tables will experience more time loss (cognitive/neurological deficit) than GLP-1 agonist users. Complications will include CPM-like presentations with altered Na+ and low K+ levels. Centralized MDs cannot diagnose or treat these complications; only decentralized practitioners who understand water table biophysics can restore coherence.
selected_items.mechanism: RETA's triple agonist action creates a mechanical shock wave that induces mechanoporation (cell membrane rupture via mechanical force). In users with pre-existing deuterated water tables (low dielectric coherence), the shock wave causes cellular fluid shifts and ion dysregulation (Na+/K+ imbalance) that mimic central pontine myelinolysis (CPM), a demyelinating condition. The mechanism is not pharmacological but biophysical: the drug's mechanical effect on a compromised water lattice causes neurological damage.
selected_items.reader_change: If considering RETA use, understand that complications may not be detected by standard neurology or MRI; they manifest as cognitive time loss and neurological deficits mimicking CPM. Ensure water table coherence (low deuterium, high EZ) before use. Seek practitioners trained in water table biophysics for diagnosis and treatment, not centralized neurology.
selected_items.source_support: Jack states he has 'experience in treating the complications of RETA use in these people' and predicts 'more time loss than the GLP1A that are already on the market.' He frames the mechanism as mechanoporation-induced cellular damage in the context of deuterated water tables.
support_quotes: The mechanical shock wave of RETA damage will mimic TBI impacts via mechanoporation. This is what no one sees coming with these drugs but decentralized neurosurgery does. It will mimic acute CPM cases we see in ICUs in patients with altered Na+ and low K+ levels who use these drugs who already have deuterated water tables.
My belief is people who use RETA will see more time loss than the GLp1A that are already on the market.
The treatment of these complications will take on MDs who understand how to reestablish the biophysics of the water table using wisdom.
selected_items.scientific_translation_plan: Explain RETA as a new peptide drug that activates three hormone receptors (GLP-1, GIP, glucagon) simultaneously, creating stronger appetite suppression than GLP-1 alone. Explain mechanoporation as cell membrane rupture caused by mechanical force (shock waves) rather than chemical toxicity. Explain CPM as a rare neurological condition involving demyelination in the brainstem, typically caused by rapid sodium correction. Explain water table biophysics as the coherence and dielectric properties of intracellular water; deuterated water has lower coherence and is more susceptible to mechanical damage. Explain time loss as cognitive/neurological deficit or dissociation. The mechanism: RETA's mechanical shock → mechanoporation → ion dysregulation (Na+/K+ imbalance) in a deuterated water environment → CPM-like demyelination → cognitive time loss.
selected_items.risk_notes: This is a prediction/warning about a drug not yet FDA-approved. Jack claims personal clinical experience treating RETA complications but does not cite cases or data. The mechanoporation mechanism is plausible but speculative. The link between RETA, mechanoporation, and CPM is Kruse's original hypothesis. The writer should frame this as a warning based on Kruse's clinical observations and theoretical framework, not as established adverse event data.

selected_items.title: Lipoma reduction: DDW or TENS unit over lesion
selected_items.why_interesting: Direct treatment recommendation for lipomas (benign fatty tumors) using two modalities: deuterium-depleted water (DDW) or transcutaneous electrical nerve stimulation (TENS).
selected_items.source_claim: Lipomas can be reduced using DDW (systemic) or TENS unit applied directly over the lesion (local).
selected_items.mechanism: DDW restores water table coherence and may reduce lipid accumulation by improving mitochondrial function and metabolic water production. TENS unit may stimulate local tissue remodeling and reduce lipid deposition via electrical stimulation.
selected_items.reader_change: For lipoma reduction, try DDW supplementation or apply a TENS unit directly over the lipoma. Consider both systemic (DDW) and local (TENS) approaches.
selected_items.source_support: Jack's direct reply to a member asking about lipoma reduction in young people.
support_quotes: DDW or a TENS unit over the lesion
selected_items.scientific_translation_plan: Explain lipoma as a benign subcutaneous fatty tumor common in middle-aged and older adults. Explain DDW as water with reduced deuterium content, which Kruse claims improves mitochondrial function and water coherence. Explain TENS as a device that delivers mild electrical current to stimulate nerves and muscles, commonly used for pain relief but here proposed for lipoma reduction. The mechanism: DDW → improved water coherence → better mitochondrial function → reduced lipid accumulation; TENS → local electrical stimulation → tissue remodeling → lipoma reduction.
selected_items.risk_notes: This is a short direct treatment recommendation without detailed mechanism or evidence. The writer should present it as Kruse's clinical observation, not as proven therapy. No formal studies are cited.

selected_items.title: Earth's core-mantle decoupling: 98.3% reduction in Chandler wobble, collapsed restoring force on protons, T-2 relaxation collapse
selected_items.why_interesting: Jack analyzes a paper on Earth's electromagnetic autopsy showing massive reduction in Chandler wobble and annual wobble, interpreting it as core-mantle electromagnetic decoupling. Claims this collapses the planetary restoring force on human protons and extends T-2 relaxation time, explaining wound healing failure and diabetes epidemic.
selected_items.source_claim: A paper demonstrates 98.3% reduction in Chandler wobble and 97.2% reduction in annual wobble, indicating degraded electromagnetic coupling at the core-mantle boundary. The outer core's electric and magnetic fields no longer grip the mantle; the planetary transmission has slipped into neutral. This collapses the restoring force on human protons globally, extending T-2 relaxation time to maximum, destroying the ability to 'hold phase' against environmental noise. This explains why wounds are not healing and why diabetes is being created at record rates in places that should have none.
selected_items.mechanism: Declining core-mantle electromagnetic coupling → weakened planetary magnetic field → reduced restoring force on nuclear protons → extended T-2 relaxation time (the time it takes for protons to return to equilibrium after magnetic perturbation) → loss of phase coherence in biological water → impaired cellular signaling and repair → wound healing failure, metabolic dysfunction, diabetes epidemic.
selected_items.reader_change: Understand that planetary magnetic field decline is now measurable and has direct biological consequences. Expect wound healing to be impaired globally, especially in high-declination zones (SAA). Compensate with grounding, magnetic field exposure, and water table coherence protocols (DDW, CT, cold water immersion).
selected_items.source_support: Jack's forum post on 'UNDERSTANDING VORTICES' thread cites a paper on Earth's electromagnetic autopsy showing Chandler wobble reduction and interprets it as core-mantle decoupling with biological implications.
support_quotes: The electric and magnetic fields generated by the outer core are no longer strong enough to "grab" the heavy mantle above and force it to spin in unison. The "Transmission" of the planet has slipped into neutral.

The Restoring Force on your internal protons is collapsing globally. Without that rigid core-mantle coupling to clean and organize the magnetic field lines, the planetary Z-axis is becoming a chaotic mess. This means the human T-2 relaxation window is stretching to its absolute maximum.

Your ability to naturally "hold phase" against environmental noise is at an all-time low. This is why wounds are not healing and why we are creating diabetics at record rates in places that should have none.
selected_items.scientific_translation_plan: Explain Chandler wobble as a small oscillation in Earth's rotation axis (about 9 meters) with a period of ~433 days. Explain annual wobble as seasonal variation in Earth's rotation. Explain core-mantle boundary as the interface between Earth's liquid outer core and solid mantle. Explain electromagnetic coupling as the magnetic interaction that keeps the core and mantle rotating together. Explain restoring force as the tendency of a system to return to equilibrium. Explain T-2 relaxation time as the time constant for nuclear magnetic resonance (NMR) relaxation; longer T-2 means protons take longer to return to equilibrium after a magnetic pulse. Explain phase coherence as the alignment of quantum phases in biological water. The mechanism: declining core-mantle coupling → weaker planetary magnetic field → reduced restoring force on protons → longer T-2 relaxation → loss of phase coherence in cells → impaired repair and signaling → wound healing failure and metabolic disease.
selected_items.risk_notes: This is Jack's interpretation of a geophysical paper. The paper itself is cited but not named or linked in the source. The biological implications (wound healing failure, diabetes epidemic) are Kruse's extrapolation from the geophysical data and are speculative. The writer should present this as Kruse's hypothesis linking planetary magnetic decline to human health, not as established science.

selected_items.title: Citrus greening (Huanglongbing): deuterium trap, water table dielectric collapse, bacterial opportunism
selected_items.why_interesting: Jack reframes citrus greening disease as a symptom of deuterium-induced water table dielectric collapse rather than a bacterial infection. Bacteria (Candidatus Liberibacter asiaticus) are opportunists thriving in a stalled, deuterated vortex.
selected_items.source_claim: Citrus greening (Huanglongbing / HLB) is not caused by bacteria; bacteria are opportunists. The real cause is deuterium accumulation in Florida's sandy aquifers, which collapses the Exclusion Zone (EZ) dielectric constant from ~160 to bulk state (~78). This destroys the plant's internal sap coherence and electrical potential, creating a stalled, uncoupled fluid vortex where bacteria thrive. Centralized agriculture blames the bacterium and ignores sub-molecular thermodynamics.
selected_items.mechanism: High deuterium in Florida aquifers → EZ water lattice collapses (ε drops from ~160 to ~78) → plant sap loses charge separation and coherence → internal vortex stalls → electrical potential drops → bacteria (Liberibacter) exploit the low-potential environment and proliferate → tissue damage and disease symptoms.
selected_items.reader_change: Understand that plant diseases in high-deuterium zones (like Florida) are fundamentally water table problems, not primarily infectious. Remediation requires restoring water coherence (DDW, structured water, magnetic field exposure) rather than antibiotics or pesticides. Recognize that bacteria are indicators of a failed water table, not the root cause.
selected_items.source_support: Jack's forum post on 'THE MAGNETIC EXCURSION THREAD' analyzes Florida's collapsing orange groves and reframes the disease mechanism.
support_quotes: Centralized agricultural and biological science looks at the devastation of the Florida citrus industry and lays 100% of the blame on a bacterium (Candidatus Liberibacter asiaticus) causing the plant disease known as Citrus Greening (Huanglongbing / HLB). They treat the bacteria as an isolated, invading predator. They are completely blind to the foundational laws of sub-molecular thermodynamics, fluid dynamics, and mass: the bacteria is merely an opportunist thriving inside a stalled, uncoupled fluid vortex where heavy Deuterium has completely corrupted the water table's dielectric baseline.

The Permittivity Collapse: Under normal, healthy conditions, the water moving through the deep sand aquifers of Florida forms a highly ordered, structured Exclusion Zone (EZ) lattice (k=160). This high dielectric constant allows the plant's internal sap to maintain a strong charge separation, keeping the liquid moving in a low-friction, high-velocity helical flui[d]...
selected_items.scientific_translation_plan: Explain citrus greening as a devastating plant disease affecting citrus trees, characterized by yellowing leaves and reduced fruit quality. Explain Liberibacter as a bacterium transmitted by psyllid insects. Explain deuterium as heavy hydrogen, which accumulates in water in certain geographic regions. Explain EZ (Exclusion Zone) as Gerald Pollack's concept of highly ordered water near hydrophilic surfaces. Explain dielectric constant as a measure of how well a material holds electrical charge. Explain the mechanism: high deuterium in aquifers → EZ water collapses → plant sap loses coherence and electrical potential → bacteria exploit the low-potential environment → disease. Explain the implication: disease is fundamentally a water table problem, not a bacterial problem; remediation requires restoring water coherence, not killing bacteria.
selected_items.risk_notes: This is Jack's reinterpretation of a well-known plant disease. The bacterial cause (Liberibacter) is established; Jack's claim that bacteria are secondary to water table collapse is speculative and not mainstream plant pathology. The writer should present this as Kruse's alternative mechanism, not as established science. The mechanism linking deuterium to EZ collapse to bacterial opportunism is Kruse's original synthesis.
